mnemonics: HOT PIE (Hash tables, Ordered Dictionaries, Tries, Priority queues, Indexing, Extensible hashing)
keyPoints: Hash tables provide average-case O(1) time complexity for search, insertion, and deletion operations
Tries (prefix trees) are efficient for string storage and prefix-based searching
Priority queues are typically implemented using binary heaps and support efficient extraction of maximum/minimum elements
commonMistakes: Assuming hash table operations always have O(1) time complexity without considering worst-case scenarios and collisions
Confusing tries with binary search trees and misunderstanding their prefix-based nature
Implementing priority queue operations with incorrect time complexity by using inappropriate data structures
